reasoning: A Minecraft Movie is currently tracking for a relatively modest domestic box office performance (approx. $55-61M opening weekend and $186M total domestic), far below the typical $500-600M+ required to be the #1 domestic calendar grosser of the year. To win, it would have to massively overperform its tracking (akin to The Super Mario Bros. Movie, but by an even larger relative margin) AND see highly anticipated releases like Avatar 3 (which will easily clear hundreds of millions in its first two weeks in Dec 2025), Zootopia 2, Superman, and Jurassic World Rebirth underperform. While there is a tiny "black swan" chance it unexpectedly dominates the cultural zeitgeist, 3% appropriately captures this long-shot probability.
